title: Gastric MALT lymphoma: chronic immune stimulation (H. pylori)
vignette: A 60-year-old woman comes to the clinic due to worsening dyspepsia and unintentional weight loss over the past 3 months. Vital signs are normal. There is no cervical or inguinal lymphadenopathy. Abdominal examination shows mild epigastric tenderness. Complete blood count and serum chemistry results are normal. CT scan of the abdomen reveals gastric mucosal thickening but no evidence of abdominal lymphadenopathy or hepatosplenomegaly. Endoscopy shows nodular gastric folds, and biopsy shows proliferation of small, clonal, mucosa-associated B cells expressing the CD20 antigen. Which of the following microbe-induced mechanisms is the most likely underlying cause of the findings seen in this patient?

clues.text: worsening dyspepsia and unintentional weight loss
clues.note: Chronic dyspepsia with weight loss raises concern for a gastric neoplasm. Combined with the biopsy findings, this points to a gastric lymphoma rather than benign gastritis.
Localizes the disease to the stomach, consistent with a primary gastric process.
Diffuse mucosal/wall thickening on CT (rather than discrete nodal masses) fits an infiltrative gastric lymphoma arising in the mucosa itself.
Nodular/enlarged gastric folds on endoscopy are a classic appearance of gastric MALT lymphoma.

clues.text: proliferation of small, clonal, mucosa-associated B cells expressing the CD20 antigen
clues.note: A clonal proliferation of small, CD20-positive, mucosa-associated B cells defines extranodal marginal zone (MALT) lymphoma - a non-Hodgkin B-cell lymphoma. Gastric MALT lymphoma is overwhelmingly driven by chronic Helicobacter pylori infection.

whatMatters: Clonal, CD20+, mucosa-associated small B cells with nodular gastric folds = gastric MALT lymphoma (extranodal marginal zone lymphoma). It arises from CHRONIC IMMUNE STIMULATION, most often by Helicobacter pylori: persistent antigen drives B-cell proliferation that becomes monoclonal and eventually antigen-independent. Answer A. Early-stage disease can remit with H. pylori eradication.
strategy: Identify MALT lymphoma from clonal mucosal CD20+ B cells, then recall its DRIVING mechanism: chronic antigenic/immune stimulation (H. pylori). Match the distractors to other microbe-cancer mechanisms: HPV E6 -> p53 degradation (and genome integration) in epithelial cancers; EBV -> immune evasion in some lymphomas. Somatic hypermutation is normal antibody diversification, not the oncogenic driver here.

options.text: Integration of microbial genome into host genome

eliminations.label: E6-mediated p53 inhibition - HPV epithelial cancers
eliminations.detail: WHAT IT IS: high-risk HPV E6 protein targets p53 for degradation (and E7 inactivates Rb), driving epithelial cancers of the cervix, anus, and oropharynx. ❌ WHY IT FAILS: that mechanism causes epithelial carcinomas, not a clonal mucosal B-cell lymphoma; gastric MALT lymphoma is driven by chronic H. pylori stimulation. ✅ Correct if: HPV-associated squamous cell carcinoma.

eliminations.label: Evasion of immune response - EBV mechanism
eliminations.detail: WHAT IT IS: EBV persists by expressing limited latent proteins that evade immune clearance while activating proliferative/antiapoptotic host genes (linked to some B- and T-cell lymphomas). ❌ WHY IT FAILS: gastric MALT lymphoma's driver is chronic ANTIGENIC stimulation by H. pylori, not viral immune evasion. ✅ Correct if: EBV-associated lymphoma (e.g., Burkitt, post-transplant lymphoproliferative disease).

eliminations.label: Initiation of somatic hypermutation - normal antibody diversification
eliminations.detail: WHAT IT IS: somatic hypermutation in the immunoglobulin variable region generates high-affinity antibody diversity in germinal centers. ❌ WHY IT FAILS: it is a normal physiologic process for antibody affinity maturation, not the oncogenic cause of MALT lymphoma (which arises from post-germinal-center memory B cells under chronic stimulation). ✅ Correct if: asked how antibody affinity/diversity is generated.

eliminations.label: Integration of microbial genome into host genome - viral integration
eliminations.detail: WHAT IT IS: some viruses (e.g., HPV, HBV, retroviruses) integrate their genome into host DNA, causing insertional/oncogenic DNA damage. ❌ WHY IT FAILS: H. pylori is a bacterium that drives MALT lymphoma by chronic immune stimulation, not by genome integration. ✅ Correct if: a virus causing cancer via genomic integration (HPV/HBV/HTLV-1).

reasoning: Clonal, CD20+, mucosa-associated small B cells + nodular gastric folds = gastric MALT lymphoma
MALT lymphoma is an extranodal marginal zone non-Hodgkin B-cell lymphoma
It arises from chronic immune stimulation, most commonly by Helicobacter pylori
-> chronic immune stimulation; answer A (and early disease can regress with H. pylori eradication)
mechanism: Gastric mucosa-associated lymphoid tissue (MALT) lymphoma is an extranodal marginal zone non-Hodgkin lymphoma of post-germinal-center memory B cells expressing pan-B-cell markers (CD19, CD20, CD22). It is driven by CHRONIC IMMUNE STIMULATION, most commonly from Helicobacter pylori infection, which recruits and chronically activates antigen-specific T and B lymphocytes in the gastric mucosa. Over time a monoclonal B-cell population emerges that proliferates in response to H. pylori antigens, then accumulates genetic abnormalities allowing antigen-independent growth and spread. Because early-stage disease remains antigen-dependent, eradicating H. pylori with antimicrobial therapy can induce complete remission - a hallmark that distinguishes this from the viral (HPV/EBV) oncogenic mechanisms in the distractors.

difficulty.label: 📚 Pure knowledge gap
difficulty.message: First Aid Heme/Micro > MALT lymphoma: extranodal marginal zone lymphoma; H. pylori-driven chronic immune stimulation; CD20+ B cells; t(11;18) in some; H. pylori eradication can cure early disease; microbe-cancer associations (HPV E6/E7, EBV, HBV/HCV, HTLV-1, H. pylori).

difficulty.label: 🧭 Understanding the question
difficulty.message: The stem asks for the microbe-induced MECHANISM. Recognize MALT lymphoma and link it to chronic antigenic stimulation (H. pylori), not a viral oncoprotein or integration mechanism.

difficulty.label: 🔤 Vocabulary
difficulty.message: Unsure on MALT/marginal zone lymphoma, clonal/monoclonal B cells, CD20, somatic hypermutation, E6/p53, or immune evasion? Tell me which.

difficulty.label: 🧲 Pulled by a distractor
difficulty.message: The viral mechanisms (E6/p53, immune evasion, genome integration) are real oncogenic pathways - but for HPV/EBV epithelial or viral cancers. H. pylori drives MALT lymphoma by CHRONIC IMMUNE STIMULATION.

difficulty.label: ✍️ Something else
difficulty.message: Bank: gastric MALT lymphoma = H. pylori chronic immune stimulation (CD20+ clonal B cells; treat the bug); HPV = E6/p53 + integration (epithelial); EBV = immune evasion (lymphoma).

keywords.front: Clonal CD20+ mucosa-associated B cells, nodular gastric folds
keywords.back: Gastric MALT lymphoma (extranodal marginal zone lymphoma)
Chronic immune stimulation by Helicobacter pylori

keywords.front: Treatment of early gastric MALT lymphoma

keywords.back: E6 -> p53 degradation, E7 -> Rb inactivation; genome integration (epithelial cancers)
Immune evasion + activation of proliferative/antiapoptotic genes (lymphomas)
Post-germinal-center memory B cells; CD19, CD20, CD22

firstAid.detail: Heme/Micro > H. pylori chronic stimulation -> marginal zone B-cell lymphoma; CD20+; eradication can cure early disease; t(11;18).
firstAid.location: Heme/Micro > H. pylori chronic stimulation -> marginal zone B-cell lymphoma; CD20+; eradication can cure early disease; t(11;18).

firstAid.detail: Micro > HPV (E6/p53, E7/Rb, integration), EBV (immune evasion), HBV/HCV, HTLV-1, H. pylori (chronic stimulation).
firstAid.location: Micro > HPV (E6/p53, E7/Rb, integration), EBV (immune evasion), HBV/HCV, HTLV-1, H. pylori (chronic stimulation).

firstAid.detail: Micro > chronic gastritis, peptic ulcer, gastric adenocarcinoma, MALT lymphoma.
firstAid.location: Micro > chronic gastritis, peptic ulcer, gastric adenocarcinoma, MALT lymphoma.

memoryHook: Nag the stomach long enough and B cells revolt: H. pylori's CHRONIC immune stimulation breeds clonal CD20+ B cells (MALT lymphoma) - kill the bug, cure early disease.
